# backend/core/middleware.py
# auto-generated explainer · 2026-06-11T10:01:42Z

## Purpose
(no module docstring — add one to improve this)

## Imports
- from `__future__` import annotations
- from `_` import os
- from `_` import re
- from `_` import time
- from `collections` import defaultdict, deque
- from `typing` import Callable
- from `fastapi` import Request, Response
- from `fastapi.responses` import JSONResponse
- from `starlette.middleware.base` import BaseHTTPMiddleware
- from `starlette.types` import ASGIApp

## Inputs
- Env vars: TENANT_ID_ALLOWLIST, TENANT_ID_STRICT

## Process
### Classes
- `CorrelationIdMiddleware`: Attaches a correlation ID to every request for end-to-end tracing.
- `TenantIdMiddleware`: Per §64.43 #7 + §41.3 — federated multi-tenant request scoping.
- `SecurityHeadersMiddleware`: Adds security headers to every HTTP response.
- `RateLimitMiddleware`: Per-IP sliding-window rate limiter.
### Functions
- `current_tenant_id(request)`: Helper for routers/services to read the middleware-set tenant_id.

## Outputs
- Return values from functions above

## Notable inline comments
- Accept case-insensitive header; fall back to new id.
- Paths that count toward the upload quota
- ip -> deque of timestamps (float)
- Prune old timestamps
- Skip rate limiting for health checks
